description: Late account in Judaeo-Arabic, naming goods in numerous colors. Dated on the verso, left side: 23 Jumād[a] I 960 AH(?) (סץ?), possibly with the hundreds place 900 represented by ץ as is common among 16th-century fragments (which the paleography supports). The letter ץ does not seem probable based on other dated documents, however, where the letter/digit faces the opposite direction– see for example: (PGPID 18355, PGPID 11059, PGPID 11323). It seems more likely that the year has been lost to damage or the scribe only wrote the day/month. MCD.
description_he: חשבון מאוחר ביהודית-ערבית, הנוקב סחורות בצבעים רבים. מתוארך בגב, צד שמאל: כ'ג ג'ומאד I 960 להג'רה (?). הפליאוגרפיה מתאימה למאה ה-16.
